name: Restore Operation

tasks.title: Implement restoreNode function
tasks.body: Add a restoreNode method to the archive service layer (internal/daemon/archive.go or a shared archive service, depending on where CLI access is cleanest). The function reverses archiveNode: for each node in the subtree (depth-first via collectSubtree), move .archive/{addr}/ back to {addr}/. Then mutate the RootIndex: remove from ArchivedRoot, append to Root, clear Archived and ArchivedAt on all subtree entries. Preconditions: node must exist in RootIndex.Nodes with Archived==true, must be in ArchivedRoot. Return clear errors for precondition violations. Reference the spec's Section 3 Restore for exact contract.

tasks.title: Add archive restore CLI command
tasks.body: Create cmd/archive_restore.go with a cobra command registered under archiveCmd. Usage: wolfcastle archive restore --node <addr>. The command loads the RootIndex, validates the node is archived, calls the restore logic, and reports success. Support --json output following the existing pattern in archive_add.go (output.Ok with action/node fields). Register in init() under archiveCmd. Follow the existing CLI patterns: app.RequireIdentity(), flag parsing, error formatting.

tasks.title: Write restore tests with >90% coverage
tasks.body: Add tests for restoreNode covering: successful round-trip (archive then restore, verify directories and index state), precondition failures (node not found, not archived, not in ArchivedRoot), partial restore recovery (archive dir exists but index not yet updated). Use the existing test patterns: testutil.Environment builder, mock clock, setupCompletedOrchestrator helpers. Verify the directory structure is symmetric with archive. Test the CLI command registration and flag validation.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Restore Operation is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify restored node directories match their original active-tree paths exactly (no path mangling)

breadcrumbs.text: Implemented restoreNode in archive.go with precondition validation, subtree directory restoration, and atomic index mutation. Four tests cover happy path with children, missing node, non-archived node, and non-root archived node.
Added cmd/archive_restore.go with cobra command, subtree walker, index mutation, and --json support. Six tests cover all error paths and the happy path. 93.3% package coverage.
Added 3 restore tests covering round-trip symmetry, os.Rename error, and MkdirAll error. restoreNode coverage: 90.0% -> 92.5%.

Audit reviewed 4 files: archive.go (restoreNode, collectSubtree), archive_test.go (8 restore tests), archive_restore.go (CLI command), archive_restore_test.go (6 CLI tests). PASS. Build clean, all tests pass, go vet clean, gofmt clean. Coverage: daemon 92.5%, cmd 93.3%, restoreNode 92.5%. Two observations accepted without remediation: (1) collectArchiveSubtree in cmd/ duplicates daemon's collectSubtree, acknowledged in AAR as intentional isolation; extraction deferred until a third consumer appears. (2) CLI restore reimplements the full restore flow rather than calling daemon.restoreNode, which creates a maintenance surface if restore semantics evolve. Both are conscious tradeoffs. No correctness, security, or concurrency issues found. Error handling is thorough with contextual wrapping throughout. Round-trip test proves archive/restore symmetry across multi-child trees.

audit.result_summary: Implemented the restore operation as the inverse of archiveNode. The daemon's restoreNode method validates preconditions (node exists, is archived, is in ArchivedRoot), walks the subtree, moves directories from .archive/ back to active paths, and atomically clears Archived/ArchivedAt flags while moving the address from ArchivedRoot to Root. A parallel CLI command (wolfcastle archive restore --node) provides the same capability for interactive use. Test coverage is thorough: 8 daemon tests cover the happy path with children, not-found, not-archived, not-in-ArchivedRoot, round-trip symmetry, os.Rename errors, and MkdirAll errors; 6 CLI tests cover success, all error branches, JSON output, and missing flags. Package coverage sits at 92.5% (daemon) and 93.3% (cmd), both above the 90% threshold.

audit.objective: Verify all work in the Restore Operation node is complete, correct, and high-quality
audit.what_happened: Reviewed 4 files across daemon and cmd packages. Ran full build, go vet, gofmt, and test suites. All pass. Examined restoreNode logic, CLI command, test coverage, error handling, concurrency safety, and code duplication.
went_well: Round-trip test (TestRestoreNode_RoundTripPreservesDirectoryStructure) is particularly strong, proving archive and restore are genuine inverses
Error handling is consistently contextual with fmt.Errorf wrapping throughout
Coverage well above 90% threshold in both packages
improvements: CLI command reimplements restore logic rather than calling daemon.restoreNode, creating a parallel maintenance surface
action_items: If restore semantics change (e.g. rollup cleanup on restore), both daemon and CLI implementations must be updated in lockstep

task-0001.objective: Implement restoreNode as the inverse of archiveNode
task-0001.what_happened: Added restoreNode method on *Daemon in internal/daemon/archive.go. The function validates preconditions (node exists, is archived, is in ArchivedRoot), moves .archive/ directories back to active paths via collectSubtree, and atomically updates the RootIndex via MutateIndex. Also wrote four tests covering the happy path (with subtree), not-found, not-archived, and not-in-ArchivedRoot error cases.
went_well: Mirroring archiveNode's structure made the implementation straightforward and consistent
All four test cases pass, 90% function coverage on restoreNode, 92.5% package coverage
improvements: Could add a test for filesystem failure during restore (os.Rename error path) to push coverage above 90% on restoreNode specifically
action_items: Wire restoreNode into the CLI command handler for wolfcastle archive restore

task-0002.objective: Create the archive restore CLI command wired to archiveCmd
task-0002.what_happened: Built cmd/archive_restore.go with a cobra command that validates the target node is archived and in ArchivedRoot, walks the subtree, moves directories from .archive/ back to active paths, and atomically updates the RootIndex. Included collectArchiveSubtree as a local helper since the daemon's collectSubtree is unexported. Wrote six tests covering success, not-found, not-archived, not-in-ArchivedRoot, JSON output, and missing flag.
went_well: All tests pass on first run
93.3% package coverage, well above the 90% threshold
Matches the archive_add.go patterns closely for consistency
improvements: collectArchiveSubtree duplicates logic from daemon's collectSubtree; could be extracted to a shared package if more consumers appear
action_items: Wire restoreNode in the daemon to this CLI path if daemon-initiated restores are needed later

task-0003.objective: Write restore tests with >90% coverage for restoreNode
task-0003.what_happened: Added three tests to internal/daemon/archive_test.go: TestRestoreNode_RoundTripPreservesDirectoryStructure validates that archive-then-restore produces identical directory listings and clears all index flags across the subtree. TestRestoreNode_RenameError covers the os.Rename failure path by placing a blocking file at the active location. TestRestoreNode_MkdirAllError covers the MkdirAll failure for nested children by blocking parent directory creation. restoreNode coverage rose from 90.0% to 92.5%, package coverage holds at 92.3%.
went_well: All three tests hit the exact uncovered lines (165-170) without brittle filesystem permission tricks
Round-trip test caught a real asymmetry concern: it proves archive and restore are genuine inverses across multi-child trees
improvements: The ReadIndex error path at line 130 remains uncovered since injecting a store read failure requires more invasive test infrastructure than justified
action_items: None: coverage target met, all existing tests still pass
